title: Printer Setup and Network Printer Troubleshooting
text:
Q: How do I set up a printer and troubleshoot network printer issues?
A: To set up a printer, first ensure it is connected to the network. Windows: Go to 'Settings' > 'Devices' > 'Printers & Scanners' and click 'Add a printer or scanner'. Follow the prompts to select your network printer. macOS: Open 'System Preferences' > 'Printers & Scanners' and click the '+' button to add a printer. Select your network printer from the list. If you encounter issues, check the printer's power and connection status. Windows: Open 'Control Panel' > 'Devices and Printers', right-click your printer, and select 'See what's printing' to check for stuck jobs. You can also run the 'Printer Troubleshooter' from 'Settings' > 'Update & Security' > 'Troubleshoot'. macOS: Open 'Printers & Scanners', select your printer, and click 'Open Print Queue' to manage print jobs. If the printer is offline, ensure it is set as the default printer and check the network connection. Restart the printer and your computer if necessary. If problems persist, contact IT support for further assistance.
